descrizione: The project isn't just a game. Once all the challenges are completed, the experience culminates with a visual journey to the Moon and becomes a veritable historical archive. Players are rewarded with an interface that allows them to explore original videos and NASA archive footage for each of the facilities they encounter (from David Scott's original experiment on the Moon to actual footage of the Centrifuge and the N.B.L. pool).

An interactive page designed for scientific outreach and virtual exploration of the Mars 2020 mission and NASA's Perseverance rover. The project is a digital laboratory where the user stops being a passive spectator and becomes a virtual operator, discovering every detail of the mission.

Radiowave NASA is an interactive digital archive of the ten most iconic audio transmissions in NASA's history, from Armstrong's One Small Step onward. Each recording is paired with synchronized, translated transcriptions shown in real time through expressive, large-scale typography that turns spoken words into a visual experience.

An interactive exploration of 11 confirmed exoplanets from the NASA Exoplanet Archive, selected to show the extreme diversity of worlds beyond our solar system. From planets in the habitable zone to ultra-hot gas giants where it rains molten iron.

This interactive archive explores how global media reported the Apollo 11 moon landing in July 1969  through visual comparisons of international newspapers, highlighting differences in tone, language, and perspective. The project shows how countries around the world reacted to this historic event, revealing how culture, politics, and national identity shaped the way the story was told.

By combining technical visualization with detailed descriptions of five NASA Earth-observing satellites, this project examines the ongoing impact of climate change. The initiative is designed to illustrate how these satellites operate and why they are essential for monitoring our planet’s health.
The Lunar Archive Explorer is a digital platform that allows you to navigate and consult the scientific data and high-resolution images collected during missions to the Moon.

Nebula Vision offers a guided journey through the Eagle and Helix nebulae to understand our Solar System. Using the Spitzer Telescope’s infrared tech, the site pierces cosmic dust to reveal scientific data invisible to the naked eye.

Exploring the true scale of the Solar System, often distorted in common representations, this interactive experience visualizes planetary sizes, positions and distances. It reveals the real proportions and spatial relationships between planets in a simplified and intuitive way.

My project aims to pay tribute to the Hubble satellite. From 1985 to today, thanks to its contribution, we have obtained the first detailed images of space, which have been fundamental for the study and understanding of the universe.

NEO Protocol is a cutting-edge interactive web ecosystem designed to monitor Near-Earth Objects (NEOs) in real time. Developed to celebrate NASA's 70th anniversary, the project transforms complex astrometric data streams from the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) into a space surveillance interface.

Beyond The Myth is a digital ecosystem mapping the evolution of astronomical knowledge, from myth to scientific proof. Through an interactive timeline, it links key figures and instruments and lets you explore the shift from analog to digital.

Spacesuit Evolution is a project that explores the evolution of NASA spacesuits from the 1960s to 2011, covering the major human space exploration programs: Mercury, Gemini, Apollo, and the Space Shuttle.

Orbit Atlas presents an interactive journey through spacecraft, orbital routes and mission environments. Users explore NASA-inspired systems through clear interfaces, animated transitions and visual storytelling focused on human exploration.

Crater Memory collects photographs, documents and mission fragments in a navigable digital archive. The project transforms historical NASA material into a visual interface where users can compare, filter and rediscover space memories.

Apollo Switchboard revisits the Apollo program through interactive diagrams, mission records and visual reconstructions. The experience connects technical details, lunar exploration and historical storytelling in a clear educational format.

Blue Planet Pulse uses satellite imagery and observation data to show Earth from orbit. Users explore landscapes, oceans and atmospheric changes through visual layers that connect scientific monitoring with environmental awareness.

Gravity Run turns NASA-inspired missions into a playable experience. Users solve challenges, control vehicles and unlock archival content while learning about physics, exploration and the complexity of space operations.

Deep Sky Index guides users through stars, nebulae and deep-space phenomena using scientific imagery and interactive navigation. The project makes distant cosmic structures easier to understand through visual explanations.

Climate Signal visualizes environmental transformations detected from space. Through maps, satellite data and animated comparisons, users observe how climate patterns affect oceans, atmosphere, ice and land over time.

Rover Tracks focuses on robotic exploration and autonomous systems used in space missions. Users inspect sensors, movements and mission tasks to understand how machines extend human presence beyond Earth.

Cosmic Sound Map transforms NASA recordings, signals and mission communications into an interactive listening experience. Audio archives are paired with animated graphics, subtitles and contextual information.

Solar Wind Lab explains complex space science through interactive models, diagrams and guided experiments. Users explore forces, materials, atmospheres and instruments with a clear educational structure.

Planetary Rings explores planets, moons and extreme environments across the Solar System and beyond. Users compare atmospheres, surfaces and orbital behaviors through immersive maps and scientific visualizations.

Data Orbit converts NASA-inspired datasets into dynamic dashboards and visual systems. Users read orbital paths, mission values and scientific measurements through interactive charts and spatial interfaces.

Launch Sequence uses illustration, typography and motion graphics to explain a NASA-related topic in a simple visual language. The project creates a clear 2D narrative where users move through information step by step.

Moon Dust Diary reconstructs important moments in NASA history through timelines, images and contextual narratives. The project connects technological progress with cultural impact and human exploration.

Starfield Coordinates guides users through stars, nebulae and deep-space phenomena using scientific imagery and interactive navigation. The project makes distant cosmic structures easier to understand through visual explanations.

Terra Watch uses satellite imagery and observation data to show Earth from orbit. Users explore landscapes, oceans and atmospheric changes through visual layers that connect scientific monitoring with environmental awareness.

Mission Patch Lab is designed as a learning tool for space exploration. Through short modules, visual explanations and interactive tasks, users understand missions, technologies and scientific concepts in an accessible way.

Red Planet Weather explains complex space science through interactive models, diagrams and guided experiments. Users explore forces, materials, atmospheres and instruments with a clear educational structure.
